title: LATAM Chronic Disease Management Infrastructure
problem: Chronic diseases (diabetes, hypertension, COPD) are epidemic in LATAM, causing 70%+ of deaths. But chronic care is episodic - patients only seen when problems become acute. No continuous monitoring, no proactive outreach, no care coordination between visits. This leads to preventable complications and hospitalizations.
solution: Chronic disease management platform with continuous monitoring. Patient mobile app for symptom tracking, vital signs logging (connected devices), medication adherence. AI-powered risk scoring to identify patients needing intervention. Care coordinator dashboard for population health management. Automated check-ins between visits.
technology: Mobile app, IoT device integration (glucometers, BP monitors), AI risk prediction, care coordinator dashboard, EMR integration, telemedicine
target_customer: Health insurers (reduce hospitalization), self-insured employers, integrated delivery networks, primary care practices
ai_type: Risk prediction models, anomaly detection, personalized recommendations
traction_signals: Diabetes epidemic: 30M+ diabetics in Brazil alone
Value-based care trends in private insurance
regulatory_notes: Medical device regulations for connected devices, clinical decision support rules
varden_fit: CRITICAL FIT - Chronic care management is core to value-based care. This is infrastructure layer that insurers will pay for. Reduces hospitalization costs. High retention, recurring revenue.
notes: Chronic disease management is complex but essential. Onduo, Livongo, Omada proved model in US. LATAM has no equivalent. Massive opportunity given disease burden.
